commit.message:
feat(build): Step 8.3 — Package resolution, resource bundling, and entry-file generation (#43)

* feat(build): add package resolution, resource bundling, and entry-file generation

Step 8.3: Implement PackageResolver, ResourceBundler, and EntryGenerator.

PackageResolver:
- Scans node_modules/@qmlts/* for package manifests
- Supports qmlts.manifest.json and package.json qmlts field fallback
- Merges QML import paths and native lib paths
- Qt version compatibility checking with warnings
- Silently skips packages without manifests

ResourceBundler:
- Recursive directory traversal with include/exclude glob filtering
- File copying with size tracking
- Optional .qrc XML generation for Qt resource system
- Dry-run mode for size estimation without copying

EntryGenerator:
- Generates runtime entry file with host creation
- ViewModel imports and registration
- QML import path merging (project + packages)
- Event bindings loading and main QML loading
- Path normalization (backslash to forward slash)

Pipeline integration:
- phaseCollectDeps now uses PackageResolver with Qt version warnings
- phaseBundleAssets now uses ResourceBundler with dry-run support
- phaseWriteOutput now generates entry file via EntryGenerator
- PhaseContext extended with resolvedPackages and bundleResult

Tests: 36 new tests (BP-41 through BP-76) covering all three modules.

commit.message:
Compile agentic workflow markdown files to `.lock.yml` using gh-aw (#257)

* ci: add GitHub Actions YAML for four Copilot agentic workflows

Compile the following .md prompt files into runnable workflow YAML:
- coverage_autofix_every_3_days.yml (schedule every 3 days + workflow_dispatch)
- auto_change_log.yml (release published trigger)
- docs_continuous_improvement_every_3_days.yml (schedule every 3 days + workflow_dispatch)
- auto_release_notes.yml (release published + workflow_dispatch with backfill_all input)

Each YAML:
- Uses github/copilot-coding-agent-action@v1 with prompt_file pointing at its .md
- Sets model: auto from engine.model frontmatter
- Grants contents: write and pull-requests: write as needed
- Implements skip-if-match via a gh pr list pre-flight step (skips agent when an
  open automation PR already exists on the target branch)
- Includes a push: paths: trigger so the workflow re-runs when its own YAML is updated
- Carries over concurrency blocks and timeout-minutes from frontmatter
- Preserves workflow_dispatch inputs (backfill_all) for auto_release_notes

Co-authored-by: carlkidcrypto <27721404+carlkidcrypto@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add actions/checkout@v4 step to all four agentic workflows

Both gh pr list (without --repo flag) and github/copilot-coding-agent-action
with prompt_file require the repository to be checked out in the workspace.
Add actions/checkout@v4 as the first step in each job.

Co-authored-by: carlkidcrypto <27721404+carlkidcrypto@users.noreply.github.com>

  so the agent can read it from its environment when deciding run mode

commit.message:
Reserve pending peer_id during outbound handshake to prevent multi-address exhaustion

Outbound discovery connections to different addresses from the same
peer_id could both remain pending after HELLO revealed the same identity.
Now peer_id is reserved in PendingConnections.by_peer_id during the
handshake (after recv_hello) for both inbound and outbound connections.

Matches stellar-core Peer::recvHello() which scans pending and
authenticated peers for duplicate node IDs before continuing.

Closes #1217

commit.message:
fix: replace 1000-block sled scan with in-memory reward counter to prevent RPC stall

The watchdog was restarting nodes because get_pool_reward_tracking() was
scanning up to 1000 blocks from sled synchronously on the async runtime
thread — blocking the RPC accept loop for 1-3+ seconds per block.

Changes:
- Add update_reward_counters(): O(n) in-memory counter update called from
  add_block; persists rewarded nodes immediately and all nodes every 10
  blocks via sled_insert_bg (non-blocking background writer)
- Add get_reward_tracking_from_memory(): O(n) read-lock, replaces the
  1000-block sled scan in produce_block_at_height, validate_pool_distribution,
  and the main.rs block-production loop
- Add reconstruct_reward_counters(): O(n) startup recovery from persisted
  last_reward_height, called after blockchain init so counters are accurate
  from block 1 without any sled scan

This eliminates the RPC stall that was causing busy_streak to escalate
to fail_streak and trigger watchdog restarts.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reject HELLO with listening_port 0 to prevent peer gossip poisoning

Accepting port 0 in HELLO caused the inbound peer's TCP source port to
be stored as its listening address, which could be gossiped to other
peers as a reachable endpoint.

Fix: reject listening_port <= 0 in process_hello(), matching
stellar-core Peer::recvHello() which rejects listeningPort <= 0.

Closes #1215

commit.message:
feat(sdk): namespace improvements for deepagents (#2386)

# Namespace factory refactor

This PR changes `StoreBackend`'s `namespace` factory to receive a
`Runtime` instance directly instead of a `BackendContext` wrapper.
Factories now look like `lambda rt: (rt.server_info.user.identity,
"fs")` rather than `lambda ctx: (ctx.runtime.context.user_id, "fs")`.

Backwards compatibility is preserved via `_NamespaceRuntimeCompat`,
which duck-types as both `Runtime` and the legacy `BackendContext` and
emits `DeprecationWarning`s when `.runtime` or `.state` is accessed.
`BackendContext` and the old-style callable signature are deprecated and
will be removed in v0.7. Tests across the backends and middleware suites
were updated to the new signature.

Deduplicate peer addresses within PEERS batch before dialing

A PEERS message containing the same address repeated N times would
temporarily reserve N outbound pool slots before the spawned tasks
discovered the duplicates and released them. This allowed a malicious
peer to transiently exhaust outbound slots.

Fix: deduplicate addresses in add_peers() before processing.

Closes #1190

commit.message:
fix(alerter): honor configured query concurrency (#1111)

* fix(alerter): honor configured query concurrency

Honor the configured alerter concurrency when throttling query execution.

A reported scheduling drift bug showed alert rules running well past their configured interval under load because workers were still gated by a hard-coded queue depth of 5, even when the alerter was configured for higher concurrency.

Create the worker queue from the configured concurrency and pass it through the executor to each worker. Expanding the queue this way helps due rules start closer to their specified schedule when many queries become runnable at once, while preserving the historical fallback for callers that do not set concurrency.

Refs: alert scheduling drift bug report

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(alerter): respect cancellation while waiting for slots

Avoid blocking indefinitely on query slot acquisition during shutdown by selecting on ctx.Done() while waiting for a slot.

Also make the default-concurrency assertion use queue.DefaultConcurrency and add a regression test covering canceled slot acquisition.

commit.message:
Reserve pending peer_id during HELLO to prevent duplicate handshake DoS

Previously, peer_id dedup was checked only after the full handshake
completed (in handle_accepted_inbound_peer). This allowed N concurrent
connections from the same peer_id to all occupy pending slots during
the AUTH phase.

Fix: insert peer_id into PendingConnections.by_peer_id during
handshake() immediately after recv_hello() reveals the remote identity.
Duplicate peer_ids are rejected with PeerDuplicate error before AUTH.
Cleanup on handshake failure removes the reservation. Matches
stellar-core Peer::recvHello() which checks getPendingPeers().

Closes #1189

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: set API ingress to INGRESS_TRAFFIC_ALL

Cloud Run-to-Cloud Run calls over the public internet URL are treated as
external traffic. INGRESS_TRAFFIC_INTERNAL_ONLY was blocking all requests
from the UI BFF and returning 404.

Access control is enforced via IAM: osrs-ui-sa holds roles/run.invoker
on the API service. Cloud Run IAM auth rejects unauthorized callers at
the load balancer before the container is invoked.
